title: senna artemisioides subsp. quadrifolia
intro: Senna artemisioides subsp. quadrifolia, a subspecies of the Senna artemisioides plant, is a type of flowering plant in the legume family that is native to Australia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to stimulate bowel movements and relieve constipation due to its anthraquinone glycosides. The health benefits of Senna artemisioides subsp. quadrifolia include its potential to alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) and promote digestive health. In herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or dried leaf tea to utilize its medicinal properties, although it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using it due to its potential side effects.
